senses.definition: A misspelling or rare variant of 'sham' or 'shan' with no widely recognized meaning in contemporary English.
examples: No common usage found for 'shans' in standard dictionaries.
The form 'shans' appears only in some proper names or as a typographical error.

senses.definition: No widely recognized sense in contemporary English; possibly a proper noun or misspelling.
examples: Used as a name in some contexts rather than common vocabulary.

senses.definition: To murmur or complain; to make expressions of reluctance or grumbling.
examples: They shant at the idea of moving again.
She shanted about the long journey ahead.

senses.definition: Variant spelling of 'shanty' meaning a sea-faring song sung by sailors to coordinate work on ships.
examples: Sailors sang a shantey to keep time while hauling ropes.
The ship's crew gathered to hear a shantey sung by the old bosun.

senses.definition: Plural of 'shantey': sea-faring songs sung by sailors to coordinate work.
examples: Traditional shanteys describe life at sea.

senses.definition: A term from Indian languages meaning peace; used in English to refer to the concept of inner or communal peace.
examples: The retreat aimed to cultivate shanti among participants.

senses.definition: Past tense of 'shanty' meaning to sing a shanty or sea song.
examples: The crew shantied while hoisting the sail.

senses.definition: Plurals of 'shanty': sea songs sung to coordinate work on ships; also a term for makeshift living spaces (shanties) in some contexts.
examples: The sailors sang several shanties as the ropes were hauled.
The town's old shanties on the hill offered cheap shelter.

senses.definition: A transliterated or misspelled form of 'shanti'; could refer to the concept of peace in some contexts.
examples: The term shantih appears in some religious texts or transliterations.

senses.definition: Plural form of 'shantih' or a misspelling; not a standard modern English term.
examples: Occasional references exist in transliteration contexts.

senses.definition: Variant spelling or plural form related to 'shanti' (peace) or 'shanty' (sea song); context-dependent.
examples: In some transliterations, 'shanti' appears as 'shanti' or 'shan-ti'.

senses.definition: A type of rough silk fabric with a distinctive texture; also the fabric’s name; can refer to the fabric or its color/style.
examples: The dress was made from shantung silk.
Shantung has a subtle sheen and a crisp hand.

senses.definition: Plural of 'shantung' referring to pieces or types of shantung fabric.
examples: The tailor offered several shantungs in different weights.

senses.definition: A sailor's song sung to coordinate work aboard a ship; a small, rough dwelling or shack in some places.
examples: The crew sang a shanty to keep time.
They lived in a shabby shanty near the dock.

senses.definition: Present participle of 'shanty': to sing a shanty.
examples: The crew is shantying as they haul the line.

senses.definition: Resembling or characteristic of a shanty (song or dwelling); ragged or rugged in style.
examples: They built a shantylike shack from scrap wood.

senses.definition: A sailor who sings shanties; a person who performs or is skilled at leading work songs at sea.
examples: The shantyman kept time with a lively chant.

senses.definition: plural of 'shantyman'; sailors who sing shanties.
examples: The ship's shantymen led the crew in a steady rhythm.

senses.definition: A densely packed settlement of simple or improvised homes, usually on the edge of a town or city.
examples: The refugees set up a shantytown on the riverbank.
Shantytowns often lack basic services.

senses.definition: Plural of 'shantytown': densely packed improvised settlements.
examples: Many shantytowns lack reliable electricity.

senses.definition: A nonstandard or rare term; no widely recognized meaning in contemporary English.
examples: The term 'shap' does not have a common definition.

senses.definition: Capable of being shaped or formed.
examples: Clay is shapable when wet.

senses.definition: The external form or outline of an object; the contour or figure.
examples: The shape of the planet is spherical.
He drew the shape of a heart.

senses.definition: A particular type or form of something.
examples: Her shape of writing is concise and clear.

senses.definition: To give something a particular form or contour; to mold or influence.
examples: The clay is shaped into a bowl.
Policies shape the market in new ways.

senses.definition: Capable of being shaped or formed.
examples: This metal is shapeable when heated.

senses.definition: Having a defined form or contour; molded or designed in a certain shape.
examples: The shaped cookie was decorated with icing.
A shaped piece of metal rested on the table.

senses.definition: Past tense of 'shape': to give a particular form or structure to something.
examples: They shaped the clay into a bowl.
